The scores of 0 are appropriate.\n- **Risk Score:** The overall security risk is minimal; the highest is 0.1, which is acceptable for such a benign utility code.\n- **Potential assumptions:** The reports correctly identify that the code does not perform any malicious actions or handle untrusted data.\n\n---\n\n### Final Justification:\n\n- The code is purely a boilerplate, autogenerated contract interface wrapper.\n- No dynamic code, external network calls, or data processing that could introduce vulnerabilities.\n- The uniformity and clarity across reports reinforce that the code is safe.\n\n---\n\n### **My own assessment:**\n\n| Aspect             | Score (0 to 1) | Justification                                              |\n|--------------------|----------------|--------------------------------------------------------------|\n| Malware            | **0**          | No malicious activity or code detected.                     |\n| Obfuscated         | **0**          | Clear, standard pattern; no obfuscation observed.           |\n| Risk               | **0.1**        | Very low; standard contract interface code with minimal risk.|\n\n---\n\n**Conclusion:**  \nThe code is a standard, autogenerated interface factory for a smart contract, with no signs of malware, obfuscation, or security risks.
